note: The VILLAGER's body (FW4). villager_body.tscn wears this record instead of embedding biped_fox.glb as an ext_resource — BodyLoader on the scene's Body node reads `mesh` and instances it as $Body/Model, behaviour-identical to the old embed. Like the player it wears the fox PLACEHOLDER today (INSTANCE #1 — data/bodies/fox.json); when canon gives the valley its people this record's mesh diverges from the player's. `look_hooks.fabric: false` records the honest difference from the player: the villager body does NOT spring its fabric (villager_body.gd never adopts spring bones — presence, not flourish). `paint: true` that CharacterPaint.apply runs on it (the CHARACTER record's body.palette tints it). The loader consumes `mesh`; skeleton/animations/look_hooks are the record system's view of the body (see fox.json's note).
